imageComparison.summary: The instructor path is editable and grounded in real assets, but the hero and verified-instructor card are too tall, causing the course workbench and teaching resources to land lower than the ideal.

majorDiffAreas.title: Hero/right verification card consume too much vertical space
majorDiffAreas.expected: The source image shows a compact instructor hero, proof strip, and the workbench/resources in the first viewport.
majorDiffAreas.actual: The editable mockup has a tall hero band and large verified-instructor card; the workbench appears lower and the teaching-resource band is mostly out of view.
majorDiffAreas.impact: The instructor workflow feels less immediately course-planning oriented and less faithful to the dense reference composition.

evidence.observation: Actual screenshot spends more height on the hero and right card before the workbench than the expected image.
Receipt notes the editable reference is taller than the generated ideal and the teaching-resource band appears lower.

majorDiffAreas.recommendedChange: Reduce topbar/hero height, make the verification card denser, shrink book-stage art slightly, and tighten gaps before the workbench.

matches: Real cover, roadmap, and logo assets load
Instructor navigation, proof strip, roadmap workbench, and resource sections exist as DOM
The academic navy/gold palette is close
uncertainties: Verified instructor eligibility copy remains product-direction placeholder text
possibleIntentionalDeviations: The real cover/CSS note art replaces generated open-book imagery by design

inputQuality: Both captures are readable and same viewport

nextIteration.focus: Compress instructor first-viewport layout while preserving verified assets.
avoidForNow: Do not invent publisher verification details or new images

imageComparison.rationale: The mockup is already editable; a focused CSS density pass addresses the visible fidelity blocker without changing content provenance.

recommendation.topFix: Compress hero and verified-card vertical rhythm
recommendation.why: It brings the course-planning workbench and teaching resources closer to the source image's first-viewport layout.
